content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile my findings:

## Comprehensive Codebase Overview

### 1. Project Type & Purpose
This is a **full-stack SaaS (Software-as-a-Service) application** called **CitedBy.ai**, an AI Visibility Monitoring and AEO (AI Engine Optimization) platform. It helps businesses monitor and optimize their brand visibility in AI-generated responses.

---

### 2. Technology Stack

**Framework & Language:**
- **Next.js 14** (App Router) - React-based full-stack framework
- **TypeScript** - Primary language for type safety
- **React 18** - UI library

**Styling & UI:**
- **TailwindCSS** - Utility-first CSS framework
- **Lucide React** - Icon library
- **Recharts** - Data visualization (charts/graphs)
- **Sonner** - Toast notifications
- **PostCSS** - CSS processing

**Backend & Database:**
- **Supabase** - PostgreSQL database with auth/middleware
- **@supabase/ssr** & **@supabase/supabase-js** - Database client libraries

**AI Integration:**
- **@google/genai** - Google Gemini API client for AI responses

**Billing & Payments:**
- **Stripe** - Payment processing with webhooks
- **stripe** npm package (v15.6.0)

**Forms & Validation:**
- **React Hook Form** - Form state management
- **@hookform/resolvers** - Form validation resolvers
- **Zod** - Schema validation library

**Utilities:**
- **date-fns** - Date formatting
- **html2canvas** - HTML to image conversion
- **jspdf** - PDF generation
- **clsx** & **tailwind-merge** - Class name utilities

---

### 3. Directory Structure

```
citedby-saasv12-audited/
├── app/                           # Next.js App Router
│   ├── (app)/                     # Protected dashboard routes
│   │   ├── app/                   # Main dashboard
│   │   │   ├── layout.tsx
│   │   │   ├── page.tsx
│   │   │   └── projects/          # Project management
│   │   │       ├── page.tsx
│   │   │       └── [id]/          # Dynamic project routes
│   │   │           ├── layout.tsx
│   │   │           └── overview/
│   │   │               └── page.tsx
│   ├── (auth)/                    # Authentication routes
│   │   ├── login/page.tsx
│   │   └── register/page.tsx
│   ├── (public)/                  # Public landing page
│   │   └── page.tsx
│   ├── api/                       # Backend API routes
│   │   ├── auth/signout/route.ts
│   │   ├── runs/start/route.ts    # Analysis run initiation
│   │   └── stripe/                # Stripe webhooks & checkout
│   │       ├── checkout/route.ts
│   │       ├── portal/route.ts
│   │       └── webhook/route.ts
│   ├── layout.tsx                 # Root layout
│   └── globals.css                # Global styles
│
├── components/                    # Reusable React components
│   ├── AICitationPreview.tsx      # AI citation display
│   ├── CategoryCard.tsx
│   ├── GenerationModal.tsx        # Modal for generating content
│   ├── RecommendationCard.tsx
│   ├── ScoreRing.tsx              # Score visualization
│   └── SourceOfTruthModal.tsx
│
├── page-components/              # Full page component modules (~14K+ LOC)
│   ├── Account.tsx
│   ├── ActionPlan.tsx
│   ├── Affiliation.tsx
│   ├── AgencyMarketplace.tsx
│   ├── AgencyOrders.tsx
│   ├── Alerts.tsx
│   ├── Analyze.tsx
│   ├── BlogOptimizer.tsx
│   ├── ClientOrders.tsx
│   ├── Dashboard.tsx
│   ├── Directory.tsx
│   ├── GenerativeTools.tsx
│   ├── History.tsx
│   ├── Home.tsx
│   ├── Login.tsx
│   ├── Messages.tsx
│   ├── Plan.tsx
│   ├── Projects.tsx
│   ├── Recharge.tsx
│   ├── Results.tsx
│   ├── RobotsTxt.tsx
│   ├── SchemaOrg.tsx
│   ├── SMCStudio.tsx
│   └── SourceOfTruth.tsx
│
├── services/                     # Business logic & service layer
│   ├── analyzer.ts               # URL analysis logic
│   ├── blog_prompt_db.ts         # Blog prompts database (~431 LOC)
│   ├── criteria.ts               # AEO criteria repository (~486 LOC)
│   ├── database.ts               # Database utilities
│   ├── generator.ts              # Content generation (~214 LOC)
│   ├── llm_templates.ts          # LLM prompt templates
│   ├── pdf_prompt.ts             # PDF generation prompts
│   ├── robots_service.ts         # robots.txt handling
│   ├── schema_service.ts         # Schema.org validation
│   └── smc.ts                    # SMC Studio logic
│
├── lib/                          # Utility libraries
│   ├── ai-provider.ts            # Google Gemini API wrapper
│   ├── scoring.ts                # Scoring algorithm
│   ├── stripe.ts                 # Stripe configuration & helpers
│   ├── utils.ts                  # General utilities (cn, formatCurrency, formatDate)
│   └── supabase/
│       ├── client.ts             # Browser Supabase client
│       ├── middleware.ts         # Auth middleware
│       └── server.ts             # Server-side Supabase client
│
├── middleware.ts                 # Next.js middleware (auth routing)
├── types.ts                      # TypeScript interfaces
├── App.tsx                       # Legacy React App component
├── index.tsx                     # Legacy React entry point
│
├── Configuration Files:
│   ├── package.json              # Dependencies & scripts
│   ├── tsconfig.json             # TypeScript config
│   ├── next.config.mjs           # Next.js config
│   ├── tailwind.config.ts        # TailwindCSS config
│   ├── postcss.config.js         # PostCSS config
│   └── .env.example              # Environment variables template
│
└── Other Files:
    ├── README.md                 # Project documentation
    ├── metadata.json
    ├── CitedBy_AOE_Criteria_50.json  # 50 AEO criteria data
    └── AUDIT_REPORT.md          # Security audit report
```

---

### 4. Key Configuration Files

**package.json** (v0.1.0):
- Scripts: `dev` (development), `build`, `start` (production), `lint`
- 17 direct dependencies + 9 dev dependencies

**tsconfig.json**:
- Strict TypeScript mode enabled
- Path alias: `@/*` maps to root directory
- JSX preserved for Next.js compilation

**next.config.mjs**:
- Configured to allow Google Fonts images (lh3.googleusercontent.com)

**tailwind.config.ts**:
- Dark mode enabled via class system
- Custom color scheme (emerald green primary #10b981, teal secondary #14b8a6)
- Custom fonts: DM Sans (default), Instrument Serif (italic)

**Environment Variables** (.env.example):
```
- NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
- NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
- SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
- STRIPE_SECRET_KEY
- STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
- API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY (Google AI)
- NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL
```

---

### 5. Main Entry Points

**Next.js App Router Structure:**
1. **Root Layout** (`app/layout.tsx`): Sets up HTML, fonts (DM Sans, Instrument Serif), global CSS
2. **Public Routes**: Landing page at `app/(public)/page.tsx`
3. **Auth Routes**: Login/Register at `app/(auth)/`
4. **Protected Routes**: Dashboard at `app/(app)/app/` with nested routes
5. **API Routes**: RESTful endpoints in `app/api/`

**Legacy React Entry** (fallback):
- `index.tsx` → ReactDOM root render
- `App.tsx` → Main component with routing state management

**Middleware**: `middleware.ts` protects routes and manages Supabase sessions

---

### 6. Core Functional Areas

**Authentication System:**
- Supabase integration with SSR support
- Login/Register pages
- Session middleware

**Project Management:**
- Create, view, and manage projects with domains
- Project overview with visibility scores and trends

**AI Analysis Engine:**
- URL analysis with 50 predefined criteria (CitedBy_AOE_Criteria_50.json)
- Scoring algorithm across multiple categories
- Generates recommendations based on analysis results

**Billing & Subscription:**
- Stripe checkout and payment portal integration
- Plan types: Free (1), Pro (3), Enterprise (unlimited)
- Webhook handlers for payment events

**Content Generation:**
- Schema.org optimization suggestions
- robots.txt generation
- Blog optimization
- AI citation analysis and recommendations

**Agency Marketplace:**
- Directory of agencies with ratings
- Order/request system between clients and agencies

**Dashboard Features:**
- Real-time alerts and notifications
- Message system
- Analytics and charts (via Recharts)
- PDF report generation

---

### 7. Code Statistics

**Total Lines of Code**: ~14,364 LOC

**Major Component Breakdown:**
- Page components: ~9,500+ LOC (largest modules: SMCStudio 716 LOC, Affiliation 566 LOC)
- Services: ~2,000+ LOC (criteria.ts, blog_prompt_db.ts, generator.ts)
- Supporting files: ~1,800+ LOC

---

### 8. Deployment & Environment

- **Hosting**: Vercel (Next.js native support)
- **Database**: Supabase (PostgreSQL cloud)
- **Build**: TypeScript with strict mode, no build errors on missing env vars (graceful fallbacks)

This is a production-ready, multi-feature SaaS platform with comprehensive authentication, billing, AI integration, and user management capabilities.

content.content:
1→# CitedBy.ai - SaaS AEO Platform
     2→
     3→Full Stack SaaS application for AI Visibility Monitoring.
     4→
     5→## Stack
     6→- **Framework**: Next.js 14 (App Router)
     7→- **Language**: TypeScript
     8→- **Styling**: TailwindCSS
     9→- **Database**: Supabase (PostgreSQL)
    10→- **AI**: Google Gemini (via @google/genai)
    11→- **Billing**: Stripe
    12→
    13→## Getting Started
    14→
    15→1. **Install dependencies**
    16→   ```bash
    17→   npm install
    18→   ```
    19→
    20→2. **Environment Variables**
    21→   Copy `.env.example` to `.env.local` and fill in your keys.
    22→   - You need a Supabase project.
    23→   - You need a Google AI Studio API Key.
    24→
    25→3. **Run Development Server**
    26→   ```bash
    27→   npm run dev
    28→   ```
    29→
    30→## MVP Features Implemented
    31→- Authentication UI (Login/Register)
    32→- Dashboard Layout with Sidebar
    33→- Projects Management (List/Create)
    34→- AI Monitoring Run Simulation (API Route)
    35→- Scoring Logic (Brand/URL/Competitor detection)
    36→- Gemini Integration (Mocked fallback if no key)
    37→
    38→## Project Structure
    39→- `app/(app)`: Protected dashboard routes
    40→- `app/(auth)`: Authentication routes
    41→- `app/(public)`: Landing page
    42→- `app/api`: Backend API routes
    43→- `lib`: Utilities, AI provider, Scoring logic
    44→
    45→## Deployment
    46→Deploy on Vercel and connect your Supabase project.
    47→

content.text:
Il reste 4 vulnérabilités "high" dans 2 groupes. Analysons-les :

1. **`glob` via `eslint-config-next`** — vulnérabilité d'injection de commande dans le CLI de `glob`. Cela n'affecte que l'utilisation en ligne de commande de `glob`, pas son utilisation comme librairie. C'est un outil de **dev** uniquement (linting), pas en production.

2. **`next` 14.2.35** — 2 vulnérabilités restantes :
   - DoS via Image Optimizer `remotePatterns` — corrigée dans Next.js 15.6+
   - DoS via désérialisation HTTP avec Server Components — corrigée dans Next.js 15.6+

Ces 2 vulnérabilités Next.js nécessiteraient un saut vers Next.js 15.6+ qui est un **breaking change majeur** (nouveau API, React 19, etc.). Voyons si la dernière version 14.x corrige ces problèmes.

Based on the search results, here's what I found regarding those security advisories:

**GHSA-9g9p-9gw9-jx7f:** This is a Denial of Service vulnerability in self-hosted Next.js applications with remotePatterns configured for the Image Optimizer, where the image optimization endpoint loads external images entirely into memory without enforcing a maximum size limit. The recommended fixes are to upgrade to 15.5.10 and 16.1.5.

**GHSA-h25m-26qc-wcjf:** This is a High severity Denial of Service vulnerability with Server Components - Incomplete Fix Follow-Up, published on January 26, 2026. The vulnerability affects React Server Components packages for versions 19.0.x, 19.1.x, and 19.2.x and frameworks including Next.js 13.x, 14.x, 15.x, and 16.x.

However, the search results don't provide specific information about the latest patch version for Next.js 14.2 specifically. The advisory page references versions 15.5.10 and 16.1.5, but there's no explicit mention of a 14.2.x patch version for these vulnerabilities. For Next.js 14.2 users, you may need to check the official Next.js security advisories page directly for more detailed version-specific guidance.

According to the advisory, the exact patched versions are:

- **15.5.10**
- **16.1.5**

The advisory does not list any patched version for Next.js 14.x. Users on the 14.x branch would need to upgrade to either 15.5.10 or 16.1.5 to receive the fix.

content.text:
## Résumé des mises à jour effectuées

### Mises à jour appliquées (build OK)

| Package | Avant | Après | Vulnérabilités corrigées |
|---------|-------|-------|--------------------------|
| `next` | 14.2.3 | **14.2.35** | 14 CVE dont 1 **critique** (cache poisoning, auth bypass, SSRF, DoS, etc.) |
| `eslint-config-next` | 14.2.3 | **14.2.35** | Alignement avec Next.js |
| `@supabase/ssr` | 0.3.0 | **0.8.0** | Vulnérabilité `cookie` (2 low) + migration API `getAll`/`setAll` |

### Code migré

- **`lib/supabase/server.ts`** — API `get/set/remove` remplacée par `getAll/setAll`
- **`lib/supabase/middleware.ts`** — API `get/set/remove` remplacée par `getAll/setAll` avec le pattern officiel recommandé par Supabase

### Bilan des vulnérabilités

| | Avant | Après |
|--|-------|-------|
| **Critical** | 1 | **0** |
| **High** | 3 | **4** (voir ci-dessous) |
| **Low** | 2 | **0** |

### 4 vulnérabilités "high" restantes — non corrigeables sur Next.js 14.x

1. **`glob` CLI injection** (via `eslint-config-next`) — Outil de **dev uniquement**, pas exposé en production. Nécessiterait `eslint-config-next@16.x` (breaking change).

2. **Next.js Image Optimizer DoS** ([GHSA-9g9p-9gw9-jx7f](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9g9p-9gw9-jx7f)) — Patch disponible uniquement à partir de **15.5.10**. Aucun patch 14.x.

3. **Next.js Server Components DoS** ([GHSA-h25m-26qc-wcjf](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-h25m-26qc-wcjf)) — Patch disponible uniquement à partir de **15.0.8**. Aucun patch 14.x.

**Conclusion** : Pour éliminer ces 4 dernières vulnérabilités, il faudrait migrer vers **Next.js 15+** — ce qui est un projet significatif (React 19, nouvelles APIs, changements de routeur). En attendant, les risques sont limités :
- La vuln `glob` n'affecte que le linting local
- Les 2 DoS Next.js nécessitent des conditions spécifiques (self-hosted avec Image Optimizer / Server Components mal sécurisés)
